images.caption: Figure 1:A three-stage view of LLM agent adaptation: fixed handcrafted systems, local component adaptation, and harness–policy co-evolution (HarnessForge).

Figure 2:Overview of the HarnessForge co-evolution workflow. Starting from a harness–policy pair, each round diagnoses execution failures, tailors the harness over planning, action, and memory modules, trains a harness-conditioned adapter from curated trajectories, and selects improved matched pairs for the next round.
Figure 4:Harness–policy compatibility matrices on API-Bank. Rows denote evolved harnesses and columns denote evolved policy across evolution rounds.
Figure 5:Representative ToolHop lineage of HarnessForge across three harness–policy co-evolution rounds.

Figure 6:Online optimization diagnostics for the RLOO and GRPO training-style baselines over the first 300 update steps.
We report actor loss, rollout reward, and policy KL from the training learner.
Curves are smoothed for readability; these traces are used only as training diagnostics, while final comparisons are based on blind-test evaluation.

Figure 7:Fault and improvement-signal distribution across benchmarks.
Each row is normalized within a benchmark, and columns group repair signals into
planning-, action-, and memory-related categories.
Figure 8:Harness–policy compatibility matrix on ToolHop. Rows denote evolved harnesses and columns denote evolved policies across co-evolution rounds.

Figure 9:Case Study 1: evidence-supported finalization for multi-hop comparison.
The child harness replaces repeated unsupported finalization with entity-level
evidence slots and support-record verification.

Figure 10:Case Study 2: API-contract repair for account deletion.
The child harness follows the required API route and emits the final answer in
the expected API-request format.

Figure 11:Case Study 3: current-query repair for retrieval-heavy multi-hop QA.
The child harness replaces stale repeated searches with question-grounded
queries and support-record finalization.

Figure 12:Case Study 4: schema-guarded multi-hop tool-chain repair.
The child harness preserves the source–intermediate–transform path while
repairing invalid date-tool arguments.

Figure 13:Case Study 5: endpoint-routing repair in TMDB-style API use.
The child harness routes to the correct TV-detail endpoint and avoids
unnecessary repeated season-level calls.
